frameworks.name: key-value memory
frameworks.one_line: distinct representations for storage (values) and retrieval (keys)

frameworks.name: similarity-based retrieval
frameworks.one_line: classical memory model where one representation serves both storage and retrieval

frameworks.name: machine learning attention/memory architectures
frameworks.one_line: modern ML systems implementing key-value separation in memory access

frameworks.name: psychology and neuroscience memory models
frameworks.one_line: behavioral and neural phenomena reinterpreted under key-value separation

core_question: Can separating memory representations for storage (values) and retrieval (keys) explain memory phenomena better than classical similarity-based models?
key_assumptions: storage and retrieval impose distinct computational demands
a single representation cannot simultaneously optimize both
key_findings: classical similarity-based models conflate storage and retrieval representations
key-value systems can jointly optimize storage fidelity and retrieval discriminability
key-value framework maps onto multiple empirical memory puzzles
interpretations: biological memory may implement key-value separation rather than single-representation similarity matching
key-value architectures unify ideas across ML, psychology, and neuroscience
